relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Let sibling-love remain.
Hospitality may unknowingly welcome messengers.
Remember prisoners as bound with them, because you too are in body.
Honor covenantal intimacy.
Live free from money-love, content in Divine presence.
The Lord is helper, so fear loosens.
Remember leaders by the fruit of their trust.
Jesus Messiah remains the same through the ages.
Let the heart be confirmed by grace.
There is altar mystery beyond tent-service.
Sin-offering bodies were taken outside the camp.
Jesus sanctifies outside the gate through his own life.
Go to him outside the camp, bearing reproach.
Here there is no remaining city; seek the coming one.
Offer praise as fruit of lips.
Doing good and sharing are pleasing sacrifices.
Leaders watch as accountable ones, never as owners.
Pray for beautiful conscience and conduct.
Prayer seeks restoration.
The Divine of peace raises the great Shepherd in covenant life.
May he equip every beautiful work and do in us what pleases him.
Bear with this word of encouragement.
Timothy’s release is shared as news.
Leaders, holy ones, and Italy send greeting.
Grace be with all.

contemplative_note: Hebrews 13 lands theology in embodied love: hospitality, prisoners, contentment, accountable leadership, praise, sharing, and the Shepherd of peace equipping every good work.
